mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60d returns with a rank IC above 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and robust economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative 5d SPX returns in > 55% of observations during early-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation measured by CPIAUCSL exceeds 4.0 percent, anticipated aggressive monetary tightening and eroded real consumer purchasing power trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY growth surpasses 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60d SPX returns falls below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly declines in real gross domestic product signal an official recession phase where corporate earnings contracts sharply, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 50% of quarters following a negative GDPC1 reading.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms a broad-based economic upturn that boosts equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy with a statistically significant alpha over 5-day horizons.
